title: Education

content:
Professional Journey I’ve worked on impactful projects spanning research, data analysis, and machine learning. Here’s how I’ve grown as a data scientist.
🤖 Interaction Research Lab, Cornell Tech Independent Researcher · New York, NY · December 2025 – Present
Collaborating on human-AI interaction research for real-world deployment in retail environments.
What I’m doing:
Architecting data models and storage strategies for multimodal data (interaction logs, sensor data, video) Designing longitudinal study metrics to evaluate robot social capabilities Conducting exploratory analyses of interaction patterns and social behaviors Skills applied: Data engineering, Research design, Database architecture, Data exploration
📊 Siena Research Institute Data Analysis Intern · Albany, NY · Sep 2023 – May 2025
Processed and analyzed survey data from the National Assessment for Service and Community Engagement (NASCE) and for CareerJam (the most powerful workforce solution that puts students first).
Highlights:
90,000+ respondents across 90+ institutions Built predictive models and dashboards for Career Jam project Mentored 10 incoming interns on data workflows and best practices Delivered actionable insights to stakeholders via visualizations and reports Skills applied: SQL, R, Excel, Data visualization, Statistical analysis, Leadership
Skills & Competencies Languages: Python, SQL, R Deep Learning: TensorFlow & PyTorch Tools: Figma, Microsoft Office Suite, Excel, Data storytelling, Research

title: Experience

content:
I love solving real-world problems with data. Here’s where I’ve applied machine learning, statistical analysis, and data engineering to drive meaningful results.
📊 Data Analytics Competition DataFest — CourseKata Engagement Analysis - Won “Best Insight Award” at a data competition. Analyzed 1.5 GB of educational engagement data to discover what drives learning.
Key Finding: Interactive content drove ~70% increase in time-on-page compared to static pages.
Tech: Python, Statistical correlation analysis, Data visualization, Storytelling
🧠 Deep Learning & Computer Vision EGC Heartbeat Classification - Built a transfer learning pipeline to classify 87,000+ ECG images into 5 diagnostic categories. Achieved 73.9% F1 score, 40% improvement over baseline.
Tech: ResNet-18, DenseNet-121, Class imbalance handling, Data augmentation
🤖 Current Research Human-AI Interaction Research - Designing data pipelines for a longitudinal deployment studying robot social capabilities in retail environments.
Tech: Research design, Data engineering, Multimodal data (logs, sensor, video)
summary:

Laia Giralt Solé — Resume (Summary) New York, NY • 518-888-0399 • l03gira@cornell.edu • linkedin.com/in/laiagiraltsole/
Education Cornell Tech, Cornell University — M.Eng. in Data Science and Decision Analytics (May 2026) Merit Scholarship • GPA: 3.93 Relevant coursework: Applied Machine Learning, Applied Data Science, Optimization Methods, Revenue Management Siena University — B.S. in Data Science and Computer Science (May 2025) GPA: 3.98 • Academic Excellence Award in Data Science Technical Skills Languages: Python, SQL, Java, R Tools & Platforms: Microsoft Office Suite, Excel, Figma, Generative AI Professional Experience Interaction Research Lab, Cornell Tech — Independent Research (Dec 2025 – Present)
Collaborating on early-stage human–AI interaction research; defined data requirements and evaluation metrics for a longitudinal deployment in retail environments. Designed data model and storage strategy; evaluated database architectures for multimodal data (logs, sensor data, video). Performed exploratory analyses to inform preprocessing, labeling, and quantitative evaluation of interaction patterns. Siena Research Institute, Siena University — Data Analysis Intern (Sep 2023 – May 2025)
Cleaned, validated, and analyzed large-scale survey data (90,000+ respondents) across 90+ institutions using SQL, Excel, and R. Built visualizations and summary tables for stakeholders; produced research reports and predictions for youth career aspirations. Mentored 10 incoming interns on technical skills and analysis workflows. Projects EGC Heartbeat Classification and Deep Learning Analysis (Sep – Dec 2024)
Built a multi-class deep learning pipeline to classify 87,000+ ECG heartbeat images into five diagnostic categories. Used transfer learning (ResNet-18, DenseNet-121) and achieved peak macro F1 of 0.739, outperforming CNN baselines by 40%. Implemented augmentation and oversampling strategies to address class imbalance and distribution shift. DataFest Competition — Winner, Best Insight Award (Jan – May 2024)
Analyzed 1.5 GB of CourseKata engagement data with Python to identify drivers of student engagement; found a strong correlation (r ≈ 0.727) between time-on-page and interactive content. Demonstrated interactive content increased time-on-page by ~70%. Leadership & Activities Division I NCAA Tennis Team, Siena University — Team Captain (Sep 2021 – May 2025) Managed team operations, schedules, and training; 4x MAAC All-Academic Team, 4x All-Conference First Team. Computing Club, Siena University — Vice President (Aug 2024 – May 2025) Organized workshops and represented the university at industry events, including Grace Hopper Celebration.
